question.text: Which of the following statements about the Public Provident Fund (PPF) is CORRECT?

answer.text: PPF accounts can be opened jointly with a spouse
HUFs are allowed to open PPF accounts
NRIs who became NRI after opening PPF can continue till maturity on a non-repatriation basis
A person can hold multiple PPF accounts in their own name

explanation.text: **The Logic:** If a resident PPF account holder subsequently becomes an NRI during the prescribed term, he/she may continue to subscribe to the fund till its maturity on a non-repatriation basis. Joint accounts are not allowed, HUFs cannot open PPF, and a person can hold only one PPF account. **The Trap:** Multiple PPF accounts are not allowed — a person can have only one in their name (plus one for a minor child as guardian). **Cross-Exam Context:** PPF rules are tested in X-A, V-A, and are important for financial planning and tax advisory. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PPF offers the rare EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax benefit — contributions, interest, and maturity proceeds are all tax-free. Maximum investment of Rs. 1.5 lakh per year gets Section 80C benefit.
